---
title: "Upload a menu file"
description: "Drop a photo, PDF, or website link. Rumizi reads it into editable items with AI."
sidebarTitle: "Upload a menu"
icon: "upload"
---

You don't retype your menu. Rumizi's AI reads photos, scanned PDFs, and even
existing menu web pages, then builds a draft menu you can edit and publish.

## What you can upload

<Columns cols={2}>
  <Card title="Images" icon="image">
    JPEG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, or GIF. Up to **20 files** per run. Blurry photos
    still work but sharper images give cleaner results.
  </Card>
  <Card title="PDFs" icon="file">
    Any menu PDF — printed, scanned, or exported from a designer tool.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Website link" icon="link">
    Paste the URL of your existing menu page and I'll crawl it. Works for
    single-page menus and multi-page sites (I follow up to five internal
    links).
  </Card>
  <Card title="Plain text" icon="align-left">
    Already have your menu in a Google Doc? Paste the text directly.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## How to upload

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the upload flow">
    In your workspace, open **Menus → New menu**. The page lives at
    `rumizi.com/your-venue/w/menu-submissions/new`.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add your files or link">
    Drag files in, or paste a URL. You can mix both — for example, upload a
    dessert PDF alongside a photo of the drinks board.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Review what you uploaded">
    I show you the files I'm about to read. Remove anything you don't want
    included and tap **Continue**. Nothing is processed until you confirm.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Wait for extraction">
    Extraction runs in the background. You can leave the page and come back
    — I save progress and resume polling when you reload. Most menus finish
    in under a minute.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Open the draft menu">
    When the run finishes, tap **Edit extracted menu**. You're dropped into
    the editor with categories, items, and prices already filled in. Edit
    anything that's off, then publish when you're ready.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What you'll see in the draft

Each run produces a **working copy** of a menu. That working copy is hidden
from diners until you publish it — same rules as any other menu edit.

<Note>
Every upload creates a **new** menu rather than overwriting an existing one.
This keeps your live menu safe if someone on your staff tests with a second
file.
</Note>

## Limits

- Up to **20 files** per upload.
- Total upload size is capped for fairness; if a PDF is too big, split it.
- Only one extraction run can be active per menu at a time — wait for the
  current one to finish before starting another.

## If something goes wrong

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="The output is missing items">
    Try re-running on a sharper photo or a higher-quality PDF. Blurry
    phone shots of laminated menus are the most common cause.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Prices look wrong">
    The AI reads what it sees. Fix prices in the editor — it's a quick
    column edit — and publish. Your correction is the final answer.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Ingredients or allergens came back blank">
    Descriptions and allergens are optional. Add them in the item dialog —
    the [menu assistant](/vendors/assistant/overview) can help write them.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="The run failed completely">
    Check that the files are readable (not password-protected PDFs) and
    retry. If it keeps failing, email
    [hi@rumizi.com](mailto:hi@rumizi.com) with the file and I'll take a
    look.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
